summary.intent: Split CI coverage collection into sharded jobs (8 native nextest partitions + per-package wasm) and add a cargo xtask coverage native command, then land the change via PR with Codex review.
summary.outcome: PR #729 was opened, all 14 shard jobs passed CI, but the final Coverage merge job failed. Codex identified two bugs (repeated --file flag construction and shard-failure guard), both were patched in .github/workflows/ci.yml, and the fix was committed and pushed.

repo: The repo uses a Codex review bot triggered by posting '@codex review' as a PR comment; the bot signals in-progress with an eyes reaction and completes with a thumbs-up or review threads.

CI topology: 8 native nextest shard jobs + 6 wasm shard jobs feed into a single final 'Coverage' job that merges LCOVs, checks thresholds, runs CRAP analysis, counts snapshots, uploads artifact, and reports to Codecov.
cargo xtask coverage merge accepts repeated --file flags to merge multiple LCOV inputs; the CI shell script must build the args array dynamically with a loop.
The repo uses cargo xfmt, cargo xclippy, and cargo xci --profile fast as gate commands before pushing.

code.finding: The final Coverage merge job needed two fixes: (1) construct --file args with a loop over shard artifacts rather than a single glob expansion, and (2) add an explicit fail-fast guard so the job errors when any shard job fails rather than silently producing an incomplete merge.

workflow: The sandbox network boundary blocked the first gh api PR creation call; a second call with network approval succeeded.
Polling GitHub for bot review completion required multiple sleep intervals (60s, 90s, 120s, 180s repeated); total wait was ~15+ minutes before Codex returned findings.
Synthetic LCOV inputs in a tmpdir are a fast way to validate cargo xtask coverage merge behavior without running a full test suite.
YAML parse validation via ruby -e 'require yaml; YAML.load_file(...)' is used as a lightweight CI pre-check before pushing workflow changes.

friction: Sandboxed network boundary silently blocked the first gh api call, requiring an identical retry after approval.
Codex review bot has no SLA; the session spent ~15+ minutes in polling loops with repeated sleep intervals before the review completed.
The final Coverage job failure was only discovered after all 14 shard jobs had already passed, meaning the bug wasn't caught until late in the CI run.
The write_stdin tool calls in the transcript produced no output, creating noise and uncertainty about whether wait commands completed.
open_items: Verify that the fixed .github/workflows/ci.yml causes the Coverage merge job to pass on the second CI run after the fix commit.
Await Codex's re-review of the fix commit and confirm thumbs-up approval before merging PR #729.
Consider adding a CI timeout or max-wait policy for the Codex review gate to avoid indefinite polling loops in future sessions.
